title: Chicken & Broccoli Stir-Fry Recipe (380 Cal, 44g Protein) | Eati
introduction: Chicken & Broccoli Stir-Fry is a high-protein meal delivering 44g of protein and 380 calories per serving — designed for muscle building and fat loss. With 8g of fat and 26g of carbohydrates, it fits comfortably into any macro-tracked eating plan. Log it in Eati to track your macros automatically and stay on target.

sections.heading: Ingredients
sections.content:
- 220g chicken breast, thinly sliced
- 300g broccoli florets
- 1 tbsp low-sodium soy sauce
- 1 tsp sesame oil
- 1 tsp cornstarch
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 tsp fresh ginger, grated
- 100g cooked rice or cauliflower rice
- Sesame seeds to garnish

sections.heading: Instructions
sections.content:
1. Combine soy sauce, sesame oil, and cornstarch as a sauce.
2. Heat wok to high. Add cooking spray.
3. Stir-fry chicken slices 4–5 minutes until cooked through.
4. Add garlic and ginger, cook 30 seconds.
5. Add broccoli, stir-fry 3–4 minutes, add 2 tbsp water if pan gets dry.
6. Pour sauce, toss 1 minute.
7. Serve over rice. Garnish with sesame seeds.
8. Log in Eati: 'chicken breast 220g, broccoli 300g, rice 100g'.

sections.heading: Calories, Protein, Fat & Carbs
sections.content:
Per serving (estimated):

Calories: 380 kcal
Protein: 44g
Fat: 8g
Carbohydrates: 26g

Macro split: 46% protein / 19% fat / 27% carbs

Protein per 100 calories: 11.6g

Note: values are estimates based on typical ingredient weights. Log exact portions in Eati for precise tracking — the app calculates macros automatically from your description or a barcode scan.

sections.heading: Why Chicken & Broccoli Stir-Fry Works for Muscle Gain and Weight Loss
sections.content:
Chicken and broccoli is the most iconic muscle-building meal for a reason — the combination consistently provides 40g+ of protein at low calorie cost, with broccoli adding volume and micronutrient density.

Macro breakdown (estimated):
• 380 calories
• 44g protein
• 8g fat
• 26g carbohydrates

Broccoli is not just a filler:
300g of broccoli provides 90 calories, 9g of protein (plant-based), 7g of fiber, and more vitamin C per gram than oranges. Its sulforaphane content has been associated with reduced muscle inflammation post-exercise in emerging research.

sections.heading: Variations and Serving Ideas
sections.content:
1. Teriyaki (400 cal, 44g protein): swap soy sauce for teriyaki and add honey.
2. Spicy (385 cal, 44g protein): add 1 tbsp chili garlic sauce.
3. Low-carb (290 cal, 44g protein): skip rice, increase broccoli to 500g.

sections.heading: Meal Prep, Storage & Time-Saving Tips
sections.content: Batch cook 4 portions — chicken, broccoli, and sauce store 4 days. Add fresh rice each day for best texture.

midArticleCta: Track the exact macros for Chicken & Broccoli Stir-Fry in Eati — type what you ate or snap a photo and let the app calculate protein, calories, fat, and carbs instantly.
conclusion: Chicken & Broccoli Stir-Fry delivers 44g of protein in 380 calories — a macro profile that makes it one of the most effective dishes for body recomposition. Add it to your weekly rotation, track each serving in Eati, and let consistent protein intake do the work.
metaTitle: Chicken & Broccoli Stir-Fry Recipe: 380 Cal, 44g Protein, 8g Fat, 26g Carbs
metaDescription: Chicken & Broccoli Stir-Fry recipe for muscle gain and fat loss — 380 calories, 44g protein, 8g fat, 26g carbs per serving. With ingredients, instructions, and meal prep tips.

faqs.question: How many calories in chicken and broccoli stir-fry?
faqs.answer: About 380 calories per serving with 220g chicken and 300g broccoli — 44g protein, 8g fat, 26g carbs.

faqs.question: How long does chicken broccoli stir-fry last in the fridge?
faqs.answer: 4 days refrigerated in an airtight container. Reheat in a pan or microwave.
